title: Suspect flees after crowd throws drinks at lone BPD officer, prompting renewed staffing worries
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: A Boston police officer was attacked with drinks by a crowd in Dorchester, allowing a suspect to escape.
The Boston Police Patrolmen’s Association cited the incident to highlight concerns over police understaffing.
Boston Police Department struggles with staffing as retirements and resignations have significantly increased since 2018.

title: What you need to know about Boston Harborfest
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Boston Harborfest runs July 2-4 with free family-friendly activities across Boston's waterfront and downtown areas.
The festival includes historical reenactments, live music, tours, and fireworks, commemorating Boston's maritime and revolutionary history.
Boston Pops Fireworks Spectacular on July 4 is the highlight, featuring performances and fireworks on the Charles River Esplanade.

title: WCVB reshuffles weather lineup with Mike Wankum’s retirement
author: John Waller
bullets: Meteorologist Mike Wankum retires, prompting a reshuffle in WCVB's weather team lineup this week.
Cindy Fitzgibbon moves to WCVB's weeknight spot, leaving her morning role with the EyeOpener team.
Kelly Ann Cicalese transitions from weekends to WCVB weekday mornings, succeeding Fitzgibbon in the morning role.

title: Boston-area restaurant chain takes Storrowing as opportunity to apologize, and plug their pizza
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: A Sal's Pizza truck got stuck on Storrow Drive after missing a clearance sign on Saturday.
Sal's Pizza used the incident to apologize publicly and promote their pizzas on social media.
Established in 1990, Sal's Pizza has over 130 locations across New England, expanding from Salem, NH.

title: As heat wave looms, more beaches close due to sewage spill
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Sewage spill in Haverhill closes multiple North Shore beaches due to unsafe bacteria levels as a heat wave approaches.
Approximately 8 million gallons of untreated wastewater have entered the Merrimack River daily since two sewer main breaks.
Newburyport Mayor Sean Reardon stresses the urgency of repairs and warns of potential public health and economic impacts.

title: Condom maker relocates headquarters to historic Lynn dairy creamery
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Global Protection Corp, a major condom distributor, relocated its headquarters to historic Lynn, Massachusetts, enhancing the city's status in the industry.
The company moved from Boston's Seaport to a 65,000-square-foot space in Lynn, citing a need for larger facilities.
Global Protection Corp, founded in 1987, is renowned for innovative products like glow-in-the-dark condoms and customized condom sizes.

title: Supreme Court TPS decision leaves Haitian community, advocates reeling in Mass.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Supreme Court ruled 6-3 to end TPS for Haitian and Syrian immigrants, affecting 356,000 people in the U.S.
TPS shelters people from countries with extraordinary conditions; Haiti and Syria face 'Level 4: Do Not Travel' advisories.
Massachusetts officials, including Mayor Wu and Rep. Pressley, oppose the ruling, urging legislative action for TPS holders.

title: In San Francisco, even $180,000 tech salaries are no longer enough
author: New York Times Service
bullets: Tech salaries of $180,000 struggle to cover living costs in San Francisco's high-priced housing market.
OpenAI and Anthropic's impending IPOs intensify San Francisco's affordability crisis as an AI elite emerges.
San Francisco's cost of living is 65.6% above the national average, with housing costs leading the surge.

title: Inspired by its anti-Belichick coach, Paraguay forged a famous win in Foxborough
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Paraguay defeated Germany in a World Cup Round of 32 match in Foxborough, winning via penalty shootout after a 1-1 draw.
Paraguayan coach Gustavo Alfaro led his team to victory despite long odds, emphasizing a 'humble and patient' approach.
Germany missed three penalties in the shootout, a rare occurrence, marking Paraguay's second knockout round win ever in the World Cup.

title: Jaylen Brown has reportedly ‘grown frustrated’ with Celtics amid offseason rife with trade rumors
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown is reportedly frustrated with the Celtics due to ongoing trade rumors and the team's approach.
The Celtics have been fielding trade offers for Brown, especially after a failed trade pursuit for Giannis Antetokounmpo.
Brad Stevens stated that Brown is valued by the team, but he was noncommittal about Brown's long-term future with the Celtics.
